text:
An angelic form of Omnimon with white wings that
flutter like a scarf. Upon realizing that some can only
be saved by fighting and defeating them, Omnimon's
sadness and resolve transformed it into Omnimon:
Merciful Mode. Photon Spread—pale blue lines of
light that resemble tears rolling down cheeks—flows
over its entire body, drastically boosting its power
for a short time. This being renders judgment upon
Digimon who have irrevocably strayed from their
path, saving them in the process. The targets of
Omnimon: Merciful Mode's attacks are always
those who originally possessed hearts of virtue.

Omnimon's Transcendent Sword has transformed
into a katana-like shape known as "Fearsome Blade,"
with which Omnimon: Merciful Mode beheads its
target with a single, reluctant stroke. Omnimon's
special move Supreme Cannon has also transformed
into Graceful Cannon, which fires a laser of absolute
zero cold.

A Mutant Digimon that can travel freely between
Digital Worlds in different dimensions. This baleful
existence devours the data of Digimon in the worlds
it crosses over to, and its purpose is a mystery.
Parallelmon appears in unexpected places at
unexpected times, and its coming heralds
pandemonium for the world. As such, research teams
consider its very appearance to be a calamity. One of
Parallelmon's special moves is Absorbent Bang, which
fires a beam from its eyes that absorbs Digimon.
Anything besides Digimon hit by this beam is flung to
some other dimension. It also has Endless Trance,
which attacks unilaterally from the countless gates it
opens around the enemy.

An absolutely conceited Insectoid Digimon that
lives by overpowering those around it. Said to be
the most arrogant of all Digimon, BanchoStingmon
holds its pride above all else. Any act that threatens
to even mar its pride will be met with ruthless
retaliation, no matter who the perpetrator. This
uncompromising pridefulness has earned it the
title of "Bancho." The carapaces of various insects
are meticulously woven into BanchoStingmon's
emblematic GAKU-RAN, giving it a sturdy
construction. Its special moves are Bloody Finish,
skewering foes with the drills on its arms, and
Depth Charge Sky, releasing mine bugs that self-
destruct on contact with the enemy.

A Pixie Digimon draped in a deep crimson tokko-fuku
jacket. The florid petals from when it was Lillymon
have been dyed a dark color, shedding the sweet
impression it once had. BanchoLillymon has the
conviction to fight for the weak in the dog-eat-dog
Digital World, and has saved countless lives from
assault by fiendish Digimon. Its tokko-fuku jacket
and the title of "Bancho" are proof of this conviction.
BanchoLillymon has several special moves that
utilize its yo-yos as weapons. Absolute Territory cuts
up the enemy along with the very space around them,
Nightmare Assemble thrusts the yo-yos at its foe and
absorbs it, and Twin Petal unleashes flash kicks from
BanchoLillymon's gorgeous legs.

A Mineral Digimon that acts of its own free will,
unlike a regular Golemon devoid of emotion.
BanchoGolemon is also one of the few Digimon
to bear the title of "Bancho." Utterly stolid and
unflappable, it is equally harsh with everyone,
never allowing even a hint of leniency into its
behavior. Electing to spend its time in the harsh
environment deep within Infinity Mountain,
BanchoGolemon is constantly putting itself through
intensive training. This Digimon is full of surprises.
Though it may never lend a hand to those seeking
its aid, it might suddenly show up to save a
comrade with whom it has exchanged blows when
said Digimon is in a pinch. With its overwhelming
power, BanchoGolemon is often able to
singlehandedly turn the tide of battle and claim
victory. Its special moves are Kanshaku Dust, which
reflects a beam fired from the Kanshakudama in
its abdomen off of Falling Stones for a wide-range
attack, and Yobori Claw Drill, which pierces the
enemy with the drill on its left arm capable of
pulverizing even Chrondigizoid to smithereens.

One of the few bearing the title of "Bancho," this
deeply chivalrous and compassionate Digimon is great
in a scrap despite its small stature. BanchoMamemon
lives a self-reliant existence, ignoring the laws of the
Digital World. It extends a helping hand to Digimon
isolated from the world like itself, going so far as to
form an organization dubbed the "Mamemon Alliance"
in which such Digimon live together. But going against
the order of the world is no easy task. It has fought
against Digimon that conform to rules under the
pretense of reorganization on countless occasions.
Even so, it devotes all of its energies toward the
Mamemon Alliance. BanchoMamemon received
the title of "Bancho" by repelling enemies without
losing a single comrade, no matter what dilemma came
along. Like BanchoLeomon, it wears a GAKU-RAN that
guards against impacts from physical attacks. That
being said, it almost always dedicates this defensive
ability to its comrades. BanchoMamemon uses its
special move Golden Rush to strike with its favorite
cudgels, the Golden Bats, and beat foes to a pulp, and
Senbon Dokkan to slam out its subordinates, the Black
Hickeys, with the same Golden Bats. It also uses
Bukkomi Otokodama to charge forward using the
prison chains hanging from its body.
